items.title: Mumbai: Western Railway's services delayed due to overhead equipment snag
items.summary: Mumbai's Western Railway faced significant morning delays today, with suburban services running 10-15 minutes late. An overhead equipment snag between Dadar and Churchgate, affecting the Vande Bharat Express, and a subsequent point failure at Churchgate disrupted services during peak hours. The benchmark 10-year yield saw a notable decrease, even as inflation in Tokyo accelerated, suggesting the central bank might consider further rate hikes. This shift indicates a strategic move by investors to rebalance their holdings at the quarter's end, prioritizing stability after equity market gains.

This has led to a wait-and-watch approach for upcoming launches, with a potential revival anticipated later in the fiscal year.

items.title: India's active credit cards cross 120 million in May
items.summary: India’s credit card market crossed 120 million active cards in May, driven by strong new additions even as spending growth slowed, according to RBI data. Net card additions rose 34% year-on-year to 1.02 million, while total spending increased only 6.3% to ₹2.02 lakh crore.

items.title: PFC, REC seek to help centre retain majority at low cost
items.summary: Power Finance Corp and REC are finalizing a merger plan to help the government maintain its majority stake cost-effectively. Two primary options are being considered: issuing preference shares at ₹10 each, requiring an estimated ₹800 crore outlay, or subscribing to non-tradable bonds worth around ₹24,000 crore. Advisors favor the preference share route, deeming it less expensive than the recurring interest costs of bonds.

items.title: RBI opens term money market to AIFIs, housing finance companies
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has opened the term money market to All India Financial Institutions and housing finance companies, allowing them to borrow and lend. Prudential borrowing limits for primary dealers have also been significantly increased. These moves aim to deepen market participation and improve liquidity, ultimately strengthening monetary policy transmission by linking short-term and long-term interest rates.

items.title: Centre eyes medical tourism boost with funding support for hospitals
items.summary: India is gearing up to become a global medical tourism powerhouse with a new government scheme. Funding will boost hospitals, colleges, and healthcare infrastructure, incorporating smart technology and AI. Mandatory quality standards and international accreditation will be key. The plan aims to establish five integrated Regional Medical Hubs, fostering medical services, education, and research, with projections showing significant market growth.

items.title: Govt extends deadline for bids under Rs 7,280-crore rare earth magnet scheme
items.summary: Industry demands have prompted the Ministry of Heavy Industries to extend the bidding deadline for its rare earth permanent magnet incentive scheme by a month to July 29. This crucial initiative, with a ₹7,280 crore outlay, aims to build 6,000 Metric Tons Per Annum of integrated manufacturing capacity for these vital magnets. Technical bids will now be evaluated on July 30.

items.title: Govt targets solar, hydrogen components to cut import dependence
items.summary: India is boosting domestic manufacturing across solar, green hydrogen, and wind energy sectors to curb import reliance. Key focus areas include solar inverters, electrodes, catalysts, bipolar plates, and polysilicon. This strategic move aims to strengthen the supply chain for critical components, addressing current import dependencies and fostering long-term cost competitiveness for India's burgeoning renewable energy industry.

items.title: US trade deal to be on the day India secures tariff edge: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India's impending trade agreement with the US is centered on gaining a tariff advantage over competitors like China and Vietnam, as asserted by Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He noted that the deal is nearly complete, with teams fine-tuning the details. Goyal stressed that the pact’s success relies on creating this competitive leverage, allowing India to thrive amidst regional rivals.

items.title: India seeks to broaden textile manufacturing footprint beyond Tirupur and Surat

The textiles ministry is in talks with Chhattisgarh, Kerala and Jharkhand to support new manufacturing clusters, an official said.

items.title: Cylinder sales restored to pre-war levels & bulk supplies raised to 50%
items.summary: The government has restored commercial LPG cylinder sales to pre-conflict levels after supplies improved, bringing relief to industrial and business users. The decision comes as import flows stabilised and domestic output strengthened, according to the oil ministry on Thursday. Bulk LPG supplies, which were earlier suspended after the disruption in late February, have also been raised to 50% of pre-conflict levels.

items.title: Nifty 50 up just 2% in last 2 years. Could a bull run be next amid falling oil prices, easing geopolitical risks?
items.summary: The Nifty 50 has stagnated with just a 2% return over the last two years. With oil prices dropping and geopolitical tensions easing, experts speculate that a potential bull run could be imminent if economic indicators remain strong.

items.title: Amazon adds new funding, lifting India AI and cloud investment to $48 billion
items.summary: Amazon announces fresh investment of $13 billion to expand AI and cloud infrastructure in India, taking its total investment to $48 billion.

items.title: Amazon CEO meets PM Modi, announces investment of $48 billion in India from 2026-2030
items.summary: This new investment is in addition to the $35 billion in funding it announced last year, bringing the e-commerce firm's investment in the country to $48 billion through 2030.

items.title: Govt facilitates shipment of Banganapalle mangoes to Singapore, farmers earn twice of domestic price
items.summary: India has successfully shipped premium Banganapalle mangoes to Singapore via sea freight, a first for commercial exports. This initiative by APEDA aims to boost cost-effective and sustainable horticultural exports. The consignment, sourced from Andhra Pradesh, met Singapore's stringent quality standards, fetching nearly double the domestic price for farmers. This marks a significant step in leveraging sea transport for fresh produce, enhancing India's global competitiveness.

items.title: JSW Infra's QIP attracts seven-fold demand with ₹50,350 crore in bids
items.summary: JSW Infra planned to raise up to ₹7,502.7 crore through a QIP of 23 crore fresh equity shares, alongside an offer for sale of 3.32 crore shares by the promoter

items.title: India initiates anti-dumping probe against 3 Chinese products
items.summary: India has launched anti-dumping investigations into imports of thermal paper, BOPA film, and certain antioxidants, primarily targeting China. Domestic manufacturers filed complaints alleging material injury from cheap inbound shipments. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies will assess dumping and its impact, with the finance ministry making the final decision on imposing duties to protect local industries. This move comes amid significant trade imbalances with China.

items.summary: Cantor said that investment flows still have "so much" allocation to the U.S., because investors recognise that the country was still one with a "rule of law."

items.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) informed the stock exchanges of a change in its senior management, with Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Sunil Agrawal resigning after more than four years in the role.

items.summary: Shares of South Korean chip giant SK Hynix surged 12% on Thursday after the company said it is seeking to raise as much as $29.4 billion.

items.title: PM Modi reviews four major projects worth Rs 30,000 crore, calls for timely completion under PM Gati Shakti framework
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired the 52nd PRAGATI meeting, reviewing infrastructure projects exceeding ₹30,000 crore to boost economic growth and connectivity. He stressed the PM Gati Shakti Master Plan for timely completion and discussed TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyan, leveraging digital tech. The PM also addressed concerns over cybercrime and digital arrests, urging coordinated action and faster grievance redressal to protect citizens.

items.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
items.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

items.title: JSW Ventures eyes ₹400-500 crore third fund, plans larger startup bets
items.summary: The early-stage investor is looking to back 14-16 companies while increasing cheque sizes and expanding its focus on fintech, AI and sustainability.

items.title: Toddle eyes up to $100 million in one of India's biggest edtech fundraises since downturn
items.summary: Peak XV-backed Toddle has launched a managed fundraising process as investors pivot towards profitable, subscription-led education technology businesses serving institutions.

items.title: South Korea’s IPO bust clouds equity markets as Chaebol structure restrains listings
items.summary: South Korea's IPO activity has lagged that of its regional peers as the structure of the market and governance reforms collide.

items.title: El Niño is set to further disrupt India's $300 billion farm supply chain
items.summary: India's crucial monsoon rains are delayed, disrupting the nation's $300 billion farm economy just as planting season begins. This agricultural setback, exacerbated by a developing El Niño, threatens staple crops like rice, cotton, and soybeans, impacting prices and potentially fueling inflation. While India's food system shows resilience, worsening climate conditions pose a growing challenge to this vital agricultural lifeline.

items.title: Deeptech startups turn to early revenue to win investor confidence and market traction
items.summary: Indian deeptech startups are increasingly generating revenue early through adjacent products, services rather than waiting years for their core technologies to mature. The strategy is helping founders attract investors, sustain R&D and build customer relationships

items.title: Deeptech, AI cornered half of InfoEdge's startup bets in the last 12 months
items.summary: Info Edge has channelled nearly half its startup investments over the past year into deeptech and AI, a shift from the consumer-tech bets that built Eternal and PB Fintech.

items.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
items.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.

items.title: The biggest wealth transfer in history is here: How the next generation will spend the trillions
items.summary: The greatest wealth transfer is underway, and heirs who are set to inherit are preparing to use the money very differently from the generations that built it.

items.title: The legal fight to get equal pay for Germany's disabled workers
items.summary: A test case is seeking the minimum wage for 300,000 disabled people who currently get paid less.

items.title: RBI says no to special exposure relaxations for state NBFCs
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has firmly rejected requests for special exemptions from concentration norms for government-owned NBFCs, emphasizing a principles-based regulatory approach. The central bank also denied calls to raise single counterparty limits for public sector enterprises. While allowing grandfathering of residual breaches and specific conditions for temporary limit breaches, the RBI has tightened rules to ensure regulatory clarity and stability.

items.title: RBI tightens rules on recovery of excess pension payments
items.summary: Government pensioners will now have stronger protection against unexpected deductions from their accounts. The Reserve Bank of India has mandated that banks must inform pensioners and obtain their consent before recovering any excess pension payments. This new directive ensures that individuals are aware and agree to any adjustments, preventing abrupt debits and requiring a written undertaking from pensioners for future recoveries.

items.title: RBI in wait-and-watch mode, no certainty on rate hike: Governor Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that an interest rate hike is premature, as inflation hasn't broadly impacted consumer prices. The central bank remains in a 'wait-and-watch' mode, closely monitoring global uncertainties and the monsoon's progress. While acknowledging improved outlooks, risks persist, but India is well-prepared for challenges. The RBI has revised inflation forecasts upwards and growth forecasts downwards for the fiscal year.

items.title: S&P cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6%, sees inflation at 5.1%
items.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in 2026-27 due to energy pressures, a weaker monsoon, and global slowdown, S&P Global Ratings reported. Retail inflation is expected to climb to 5.1% this fiscal year. Despite near-term challenges, GDP growth is anticipated to rebound to 7.2% in 2027-28, with oil prices expected to stabilize.

items.title: Goyal heads to UK ahead of India-UK trade pact rollout
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal's UK visit from June 25-27 precedes the July 15 implementation of the India-UK CETA and Double Contribution Convention. Discussions with UK's Business and Trade Secretary Peter Kyle will focus on regulatory alignment, customs coordination, and operationalizing tariff liberalisation for Indian exports. The talks also aim to boost professional mobility and achieve a bilateral trade target of $120 billion by 2030.

items.title: India, US inch closer to interim trade pact after fresh talks
items.summary: India and the US are nearing an interim trade deal, with both nations reporting significant progress in recent negotiations. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer concluded two days of talks, aiming to finalize the first phase of a bilateral trade agreement. Discussions focused on market access, digital trade, and non-tariff barriers, with both sides committed to a balanced pact delivering tangible benefits.

items.title: Exporters urge govt not to impose curbs on onions
items.summary: Indian onion exporters are urging the government against imposing export curbs, citing a surge in wholesale prices at Lasalgaon market. Delayed sowing and adverse weather have impacted production, pushing prices up. Exporters warn that restrictions could jeopardize markets painstakingly regained after previous bans, potentially shifting buyers to competing nations and damaging India's trade reputation.

items.title: Baroness Mone among individuals sued to recover PPE Medpro millions
items.summary: The Baroness and her husband Doug Barrowman are among individuals being sued in a bid to recover some of the money owed to the government by the business.

items.title: RBI might have just handed Tata Sons a leeway on listing
items.summary: The RBI has seemingly removed a contentious definition of indirect public funds received by non-bank financiers, in what can be seen as a breather to Tata Sons on a mandatory listing.

items.title: Goyal meets exporters to discuss ways to boost shipments, leverage FTAs
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with exporters to strategize on boosting outbound shipments and maximizing opportunities through Free Trade Agreements. Discussions focused on strengthening MSME support, fostering innovation, improving export facilitation, and expanding India's global value chain role. The meeting reviewed strategies to enhance the export ecosystem, diversify markets, and boost trade competitiveness, with councils presenting their progress on key performance indicators.

items.title: FTA impact, import substitution, rupee payments at SEZ reforms panel meet June 30
items.summary: A government committee reviewing India’s Special Economic Zone (SEZ) policy will hold a stakeholder meeting on June 30 to examine how free trade agreements are affecting export hubs and to explore reforms aimed at boosting import substitution.

items.title: EXL to acquire AI data firm iMerit for $310 million
items.summary: The acquisition comes as enterprises increasingly move AI projects from experimentation to large-scale deployment, driving demand for high-quality data, model training, and evaluation services

items.title: India targets $100 billion textile exports by 2030, focus on sustainability, FTAs
items.summary: India is aiming to become a global textile powerhouse, targeting $100 billion in exports by 2030. The strategy emphasizes sustainability, including recycling and environmental compliance, alongside leveraging free trade agreements. Key initiatives involve digital product passports, supporting MSMEs, and enhancing product-market strategies. The focus is on translating policy into action across all levels to boost the sector and promote 'Brand India' internationally.

items.title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass to resume after 6 yrs
items.summary: Border trade between India and Tibet is set to resume through the Lipulekh pass on June 26, after a six-year halt due to the pandemic. The first batch of 26 Indian traders, including helpers, will enter Chinese territory. A customs office is operational, and preparations are underway for a second batch. Traders anticipate logistical hurdles and will assess goods stored for years before seeking government aid for potential losses.

items.title: Nila Spaces and IKEA partner for B2B push, eye growth in India’s emerging ready-to-live homes market
items.summary: IKEA collaborates with Nila Spaces to tap into India's burgeoning ready-to-move-in home market. The partnership aims to meet the demand for functional homes tailored to modern living needs, signifying a shift in real estate toward convenience and personalization for homebuyers.

items.title: Elon Musk is no longer a trillionaire, but his $957 billion net worth still makes him world's richest person
items.summary: Elon Musk's net worth has dropped to $957 billion after a SpaceX and Tesla share slide, ending his brief run as the world's first trillionaire.

items.title: Amazon scales rapid delivery ‘Now’ to 300+ cities; launches ‘Sammaan’ program for delivery partner welfare
items.summary: Things needed quickly, like groceries, shampoo, baby products, get picked and delivered just minutes after being ordered, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said, adding that “customers are loving it...”

items.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
items.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.

items.title: Sublime enters travel retail with first airport store at Bengaluru Airport Terminal 2
items.summary: Sublime has opened its first airport store at Bengaluru Airport's Terminal 2, offering luxury teas, dry fruits, spices, honey, crockery and curated gifting options.

items.title: Indigo Paints presses the accelerator, puts growth ahead of margins
items.summary: Indigo Paints will accept a 2-2.5 percentage point hit to gross margins as it ramps up spending on trade and influencer marketing to chase market share, joining Berger, JSW Dulux and Birla Opus in prioritizing growth.

items.title: MUFG consolidates Ganesha Fund with Dragon, eyes $600 million second fund
items.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group will merge Ganesha Fund's operations with Dragon Funds, enhancing investment processes in India. The new fund aims to raise $600 million, focusing on growth-stage technology companies globally, especially in India.

items.title: Govt invites applications for BSNL CMD post, including private sector candidates
items.summary: BSNL has been without a full-time CMD for two years and reported significant financial losses. The deadline for applications is July 22, 2024.
